author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Famitsu’s most wanted games – June 7, 2026 – Pokemon Winds and Waves again on top

It’s time for the latest Famitsu’s most wanted games chart outlining what Japanese gamers are looking forward to based on reader votes.  As we’ve been seeing as of late, Pokemon Wins and Waves are winning here with almost 600 votes. The other little tidbit is that Granblue Fantasy: Relink – Endless Ragnarok appears for the first time, though all the...

The post Famitsu’s most wanted games – June 7, 2026 – Pokemon Winds and Waves again on top appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Nintendo Switch eShop latest sales: lowest prices ever for Spyro Reignited Trilogy, Yakuza Kiwami, more

A bunch of new sales are up and running on the North American Nintendo Switch eShop. These include the lowest prices ever for Spyro Reignited Trilogy, Yakuza Kiwami, and more. Here’s the full roundup: – 1000xResist – $11.99 (was $19.99) – 30XX – $8.99 (was $19.99) – Ace Attorney Investigations Collection – $23.99 (was $39.99) – Adventure of Samsara –...

The post Nintendo Switch eShop latest sales: lowest prices ever for Spyro Reignited Trilogy, Yakuza Kiwami, more appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Nintendo pre-order updates – June 7, 2026 – Rayman Legends Retold, Toy Story, more

Below are the latest Nintendo products that can be currently pre-ordered at retailers for the week of June 7, 2026: United States Switch 2 The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales – June 18 – Amazon, Best Buy, GameStop Destroy All Humans – June 23 – Amazon, Best Buy Star Fox – June 25 – Amazon, Best Buy Super Meat...

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Rumor: Nintendo has a new Wario game on the way

Rumor has it that Nintendo has some sort of new Wario game in development. The speculation comes from noarmsandnolegs, who made the claim on social media. We don’t have any details on the project currently. noarmsandnolegs also said that if there is a Nintendo Direct week – which could take place as early as Tuesday – the Wario game isn’t...

The post Rumor: Nintendo has a new Wario game on the way appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Valheim receives Nintendo Switch 2 release date, new trailer

Valheim now has its release date on Nintendo Switch 2. Today, it was announced that the title arrives on September 9, 2026. You may recall that Valheim was confirmed for Nintendo Switch 2 during a Nintendo Direct. When Valheim comes to Nintendo Switch 2, this will be a part of the game’s proper 1.0 launch. A slew of details can...

The post Valheim receives Nintendo Switch 2 release date, new trailer appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Sea of Stars gets a Nintendo Switch 2 version

Sea of Stars is receiving a dedicated Nintendo Switch 2 version. That’s according to a listing on the Japanese Nintendo eShop. It’s already live on the store with other regions to follow – likely tomorrow. Sea of Stars previously launched on Nintendo Switch. Note that the new release is not a Nintendo Switch 2 Edition, so save data will not...

The post Sea of Stars gets a Nintendo Switch 2 version appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Thief: The Dark Project Remastered announced for Nintendo Switch 2, Switch

During the PC Gamer Show today, it was announced that Thief: The Dark Project Remastered is coming to both Nintendo Switch 2 and Switch. A release is planned for this winter. Eidos originally made Thief: The Dark Project in 1998. The first-person stealth game will now be updated in the KEX Engine with the base game plus the additional missions,...

The post Thief: The Dark Project Remastered announced for Nintendo Switch 2, Switch appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Cassette Beasts 2002, a follow-up to the original Cassette Beasts, revealed for Nintendo Switch 2

Cassette Beasts 2002 is in development for Nintendo Switch 2, Raw Fury and Cassette Beasts 2002 have revealed. The game’s release date will be shared later. This is a follow-up to the original Cassette Beasts. The open-world RPG previously came to Nintendo Switch in 2023. In the follow-up, it’ll be possible to fuse with over 240 beasts. Additionally, any two...

The post Cassette Beasts 2002, a follow-up to the original Cassette Beasts, revealed for Nintendo Switch 2 appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Crazy Taxi: World Tour uses generative AI during development

You can add Crazy Taxi: World Tour to the lineup of games that’s using generative AI during development. That piece of information comes from the game’s official Steam page. SEGA just announced the game, and in the description on the store page, it includes a note about AI usage. SEGA describes its implementation as follows: “At SEGA Corporation, we utilize...

The post Crazy Taxi: World Tour uses generative AI during development appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Castlevania: Belmont’s Curse won’t be on Nintendo Switch at launch

Konami and Evil Empire just set the release date for Castlevania: Belmont’s Curse today, but those interested in the Nintendo Switch version will need to wait a bit longer for the game. On most platforms, the title is coming on October 15, 2026. However, in the most recent trailer, Konami confirms that the Nintendo Switch version is launching in 2026. What...

The post Castlevania: Belmont’s Curse won’t be on Nintendo Switch at launch appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

text:
List of all Nintendo Switch demos

Thousands of games are currently available on Nintendo Switch, and there are a ton of demos out there for many of them. Not just Nintendo, but also third-parties are offering demos. We’ve now put together a comprehensive list of what’s out there. Since our reader base is English-speaking, that’s what we’re focusing on here. In other words, we’re highlighting Switch...

The post List of all Nintendo Switch demos appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Spyro: A Realm Beyond dev wants Spyro to live on for a long time, hopes this is the start of “many new games”

We’re finally getting a brand new Spyro game with next year’s Spyro: A Realm Beyond, and developer Toys for Bob has high hopes for the future. Studio head Paul Yan touched on what this new title means going forward. He started out by saying that the company wants the character “to live on for a long time” and wants “many...

The post Spyro: A Realm Beyond dev wants Spyro to live on for a long time, hopes this is the start of “many new games” appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Smalland 2: Lost Realms announced for Nintendo Switch 2

Maximum Entertainment today announced Smalland 2: Lost Realms, and confirmed plans to release it on Nintendo Switch 2. The game launches in 2027. Smalland 2: Lost Realms is a sequel to the beloved miniature-scale open-world survival adventure game. Players can look forward to a larger world, deeper progression systems, creature companionships, and more, all while maintaining 10-player co-op. Here’s some...

The post Smalland 2: Lost Realms announced for Nintendo Switch 2 appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Visual novel Pitstop in Purgatory out on Nintendo Switch this week

Pitstop in Purgatory is releasing on Nintendo Switch this week, Ratalaika Games and Tymedust Games have announced. This one is due out on Friday, June 12. Pitstop in Purgatory is a visual novel that will have players solving a mystery. You’ll be playing as Astrid, the game’s heroine who has met her demise – so you’ll need to find out...

The post Visual novel Pitstop in Purgatory out on Nintendo Switch this week appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Sea of Stars “Sunset Edition” update announced, the game’s final content update

Today, Sabotage provided details on the final update for Sea of Stars, titled “Sunset Edition”. It is available starting today. The Sunset Edition will be the last content update for the game. It’ll bring along an intro cinematic, the expansion of a key flashback, and difficulty balancing. Here’s some additional information: The main thing it brings to the game is...

The post Sea of Stars “Sunset Edition” update announced, the game’s final content update appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Sea of Stars gets a Nintendo Switch 2 version [update: out today worldwide, saves transfer, new details]

June 7: Sea of Stars is receiving a dedicated Nintendo Switch 2 version. That’s according to a listing on the Japanese Nintendo eShop. It’s already live on the store with other regions to follow – likely tomorrow. Sea of Stars previously launched on Nintendo Switch. Note that the new release is not a Nintendo Switch 2 Edition, so save data...

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Nintendo Direct announced for June 9

A Nintendo Direct is officially happening tomorrow, June 9, the company just announced. It will air at 7 AM PT / 10 AM ET / 3 PM in the UK / 4 PM in Europe. According to Nintendo, the presentation will be about 50 minutes of long. It will focus on games for the second half of 2026 for both...

The post Nintendo Direct announced for June 9 appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Nintendo hit with €35 million fine over Switch Joy-Con drift

Following a French investigation the Directorate General for Competition, Consumer Affairs and Fraud Control (DGCCRF), Nintendo will be forced to pay €35 million over Switch Joy-Con drift. This all started following a complaint from UFC-Que Choisir in September 2020. The National Investigation Service (SNE) of the DGCCRF determined that Nintendo of Europe had committed a misleading commercial practice from 2018...

The post Nintendo hit with €35 million fine over Switch Joy-Con drift appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Spyro: A Realm Beyond dev talks about Spyro’s new design

Spyro has an updated look in Spyro: A Realm Beyond, and Toys For Bob studio head Paul Yan has discussed the new design. The character hasn’t seen a major overhaul, but there are a few updates. As one example, there are differences with his hair and he has a wider wingspan.  According to Yan, Spyro “went through countless iterations” for...

The post Spyro: A Realm Beyond dev talks about Spyro’s new design appeared first on Nintendo Everything.

author: Nintendo Everything
text:
Nintendo Switch 2 and Switch NES, SNES, and Game Boy games reportedly getting challenges

Major functionality is apparently planned for NES, SNES, and Game Boy games on Switch 2 and Switch with Nintendo gearing up to release a challenges feature. You may have noticed that the apps for these platforms have been updated. You can download a patch to move the NES app to version 9.0.0. The SNES app was bumped up to version...

The post Nintendo Switch 2 and Switch NES, SNES, and Game Boy games reportedly getting challenges appeared first on Nintendo Everything.
